connection_attempt.c(3) Library Functions Manual connection_attempt.c(3)

NAME

connection_attempt.c - Connection state machine and attempt logic for TCP/WebSocket clients.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void session_client_like_set_websocket_client (struct websocket_client *ws_client)
const char * connection_state_name (connection_state_t state)
Get human-readable state name for logging. asciichat_error_t connection_context_init (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Initialize connection attempt context. void connection_context_cleanup (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Cleanup connection attempt context. asciichat_error_t connection_state_transition (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, connection_state_t new_state)
Transition to next connection state with validation. bool connection_check_timeout (const connection_attempt_context_t *ctx)
Check if connection attempt has exceeded timeout. asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_tcp (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, const char *server_address, uint16_t server_port, struct tcp_client *pre_created_tcp_client)
Attempt direct TCP connection. asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_websocket (connection_attempt_context_t *ctx, const char *ws_url)
Attempt WebSocket connection (ws:// or wss://).

Detailed Description

Connection state machine and attempt logic for TCP/WebSocket clients.

Implements direct TCP and WebSocket connections for client mode. Manages connection state transitions, timeouts, and transport lifecycle.

Features:

  • Direct TCP connection to server
  • WebSocket connection support
  • Timeout management
  • Crypto handshake integration
  • ACIP transport creation
  • Proper resource cleanup

Integration Points:

  • Called from src/client/main.c connection loop
  • Returns active transport when connection succeeds

Date

January 2026

Version

2.0

Definition in file connection_attempt.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t connection_attempt_tcp (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx, const char * server_address, uint16_t server_port, struct tcp_client * pre_created_tcp_client)

Attempt direct TCP connection. Connects to server via TCP, performs crypto handshake if enabled, and creates ACIP transport for protocol communication.

Definition at line 182 of file connection_attempt.c.

183                                                                                                           {
184   log_info("=== connection_attempt_tcp CALLED: address='%s', port=%u, pre_created=%p ===", server_address, server_port,
185            (void *)pre_created_tcp_client);
186 
187   if (!ctx || !server_address) {
188     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
189   }
190 
191   // Check if shutdown was requested before attempting connection (via callback if available)
192   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
193     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection attempt aborted due to shutdown request");
194   }
195 
196   // Check for WebSocket URL or TCP URL - handle separately from bare TCP addresses
197   log_debug("connection_attempt_tcp: server_address='%s', port=%u", server_address, server_port);
198 
199   // Check for TCP URL scheme (tcp://) and parse it to extract host and port
200   char tcp_address_buf[512] = {0};
201   const char *effective_address = server_address;
202   uint16_t effective_port = server_port;
203 
204   if (url_is_tcp(server_address)) {
205     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
206     if (url_parse(server_address, &url_parts) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
207       log_debug("TCP URL parsed: host=%s, port=%d, scheme=%s", url_parts.host, url_parts.port, url_parts.scheme);
208       // Copy host to local buffer since url_parts will be destroyed
209       SAFE_STRNCPY(tcp_address_buf, url_parts.host, sizeof(tcp_address_buf) - 1);
210       effective_address = tcp_address_buf;
211       effective_port = (url_parts.port > 0) ? (uint16_t)url_parts.port : server_port;
212       log_debug("TCP URL converted to address='%s', port=%u", effective_address, effective_port);
213       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
214     } else {
215       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Failed to parse TCP URL: %s", server_address);
216     }
217   }
218 
219   if (url_is_websocket(server_address)) {
220     // WebSocket connection path
221     const char *ws_url = server_address;
222 
223     // Parse for debug logging and server IP extraction
224     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
225     if (url_parse(server_address, &url_parts) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
226       log_debug("WebSocket URL parsed: host=%s, port=%d, scheme=%s", url_parts.host, url_parts.port, url_parts.scheme);
227 
228       // Set server IP for crypto handshake
229       // This ensures the crypto context has proper server address info for known_hosts verification
230       APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(server_connection_set_ip, url_parts.host);
231       log_debug("Set server IP to %s from WebSocket URL", url_parts.host);
232     }
233 
234     log_info("Attempting WebSocket connection to %s", ws_url);
235 
236     // Transition to attempting state
237     asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
238     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
239       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
240       return result;
241     }
242 
243     // Set timeout for this attempt
244     ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
245     ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP; // Use same timeout as TCP
246 
247     // Compute crypto mode from CLI options
248     // Determine if client has authentication material (keys or password)
249     bool has_auth_material =
250         (GET_OPTION(encrypt_key)[0] != '\0' || GET_OPTION(num_identity_keys) > 0 || GET_OPTION(password)[0] != '\0');
251     bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
252     bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
253 
254     uint8_t crypto_mode;
255     if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && !has_auth_material)
256       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_ENCRYPT; // default: encrypt only
257     else if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
258       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_FULL; // full: encrypt + auth
259     else if (no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
260       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_AUTH; // auth-only mode
261     else
262       crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE; // no crypto
263 
264     log_debug("WebSocket crypto mode computed: 0x%02x (encrypt=%d, auth=%d)", crypto_mode,
265               ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(crypto_mode), ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(crypto_mode));
266 
267     // Set server IP and port for crypto handshake known_hosts verification
268     // This is required for the crypto layer to properly validate server identity
269     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(server_connection_set_ip, url_parts.host);
270     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_INT(server_connection_set_port, url_parts.port);
271     log_debug("Set server IP=%s, port=%d for WebSocket crypto handshake", url_parts.host, url_parts.port);
272 
273     // Set crypto mode before initialization via callback
274     // NOTE: Always initialize crypto context, even in NONE mode!
275     // The server always sends KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packets, and the context
276     // must be initialized to handle them (even if in disabled/none mode).
277     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
278 
279     log_debug("Initializing crypto context for WebSocket with mode 0x%02x...", crypto_mode);
280 
281     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT8(client_crypto_set_mode, crypto_mode);
282 
283     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
284       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
285       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
286       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
287     }
288     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
289 
290     // Get crypto context if ready
291     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
292       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
293     }
294 
295     // Create WebSocket client instance
296     websocket_client_t *ws_client = websocket_client_create("discovery_connection");
297     if (!ws_client) {
298       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket client");
299       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
300       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
301       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket client creation failed");
302     }
303 
304     // Connect via WebSocket
305     acip_transport_t *transport = websocket_client_connect(ws_client, ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
306     if (!transport) {
307       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
308       websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
309       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
310       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
311       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket connection failed");
312     }
313 
314     // CRITICAL: Wait for the WebSocket handshake to actually complete
315     // The transport is created but the TLS/crypto handshake happens in the service thread
316     log_info("WebSocket transport created, waiting for TLS handshake completion...");
317     const int MAX_HANDSHAKE_WAIT_ITERATIONS = 300; // 300 * 50ms = 15 seconds
318     int handshake_wait_count = 0;
319     while (!acip_transport_is_connected(transport) && handshake_wait_count < MAX_HANDSHAKE_WAIT_ITERATIONS) {
320       platform_sleep_us(50000); // Sleep 50ms between checks
321       handshake_wait_count++;
322       if (handshake_wait_count % 20 == 0) {
323         log_info("WebSocket handshake waiting... (%d seconds elapsed)", handshake_wait_count / 20);
324       }
325     }
326 
327     if (!acip_transport_is_connected(transport)) {
328       log_error("WebSocket TLS handshake failed or timed out after 15 seconds");
329       websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
330       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
331       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
332       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket handshake timeout");
333     }
334 
335     log_info("WebSocket connection established to %s", ws_url);
336     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
337     ctx->active_transport = transport;
338     ctx->ws_client_instance = ws_client;
339 
340     // Update global websocket client so session_client_like_run() detects network mode
341     session_client_like_set_websocket_client(ws_client);
342 
343     url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
344     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
345   }
346 
347   // TCP connection path (original logic)
348   log_info("Attempting TCP connection to %s:%u (3s timeout)", server_address, server_port);
349 
350   // Transition to attempting state
351   asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
352   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
353     return result;
354   }
355 
356   // Use pre-created TCP client if provided, otherwise create one
357   tcp_client_t *tcp_client = pre_created_tcp_client;
358   bool created_tcp_client = false;
359   if (!tcp_client) {
360     tcp_client = tcp_client_create();
361     created_tcp_client = true;
362     if (!tcp_client) {
363       log_error("Failed to create TCP client");
364       connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
365       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "TCP client creation failed");
366     }
367     log_debug("Created TCP client locally (not pre-created by framework)");
368   } else {
369     log_debug("Using pre-created TCP client from framework");
370   }
371 
372   // Set timeout for this attempt
373   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
374   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
375 
376   // Attempt TCP connection (reconnect_attempt is 0-based, convert for tcp_client_connect)
377   // Use effective_address/port which may be parsed from tcp:// URL scheme
378   int tcp_result = tcp_client_connect(tcp_client, effective_address, effective_port, (int)ctx->reconnect_attempt,
379                                       ctx->reconnect_attempt == 0, ctx->reconnect_attempt > 0);
380 
381   if (tcp_result != 0) {
382     log_debug("TCP connection failed (tcp_client_connect returned %d)", tcp_result);
383     if (created_tcp_client) {
384       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
385     }
386     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
387     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "TCP connection failed after %u attempts", ctx->reconnect_attempt);
388   }
389 
390   // Extract socket from TCP client for crypto handshake
391   socket_t sockfd = tcp_client_get_socket(tcp_client);
392   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
393     log_error("Failed to get socket from TCP client");
394     if (created_tcp_client) {
395       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
396     }
397     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid socket after TCP connection");
398   }
399 
400   // Note: TCP client stores server IP in tcp_client->server_ip for potential crypto context use
401   if (tcp_client->server_ip[0] != '\0') {
402     log_debug("Server IP available from TCP client: %s", tcp_client->server_ip);
403     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_STR(server_connection_set_ip, tcp_client->server_ip);
404   } else {
405     log_warn("TCP client did not populate server_ip field");
406   }
407 
408   // Compute crypto mode from CLI options
409   // Determine if client has authentication material (keys or password)
410   bool has_auth_material =
411       (GET_OPTION(encrypt_key)[0] != '\0' || GET_OPTION(num_identity_keys) > 0 || GET_OPTION(password)[0] != '\0');
412   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
413   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
414 
415   uint8_t crypto_mode;
416   if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && !has_auth_material)
417     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_ENCRYPT; // default: encrypt only
418   else if (!no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
419     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_FULL; // full: encrypt + auth
420   else if (no_encrypt && !no_auth && has_auth_material)
421     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_AUTH; // auth-only mode
422   else
423     crypto_mode = ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE; // no crypto
424 
425   log_debug("TCP crypto mode computed: 0x%02x (encrypt=%d, auth=%d)", crypto_mode, ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(crypto_mode),
426             ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(crypto_mode));
427 
428   // Setup crypto operations via callbacks
429   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
430 
431   if (crypto_mode != ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE) {
432     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT8(client_crypto_set_mode, crypto_mode);
433 
434     // Initialize crypto context if mode requires handshake (not ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE)
435     // This must happen AFTER setting server IP, as crypto init reads server IP/port
436     log_debug("Initializing crypto context...");
437     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
438       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
439       if (created_tcp_client) {
440         tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
441       }
442       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
443     }
444     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
445 
446     // Perform crypto handshake with server
447     log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with server...");
448     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT_SOCKET(client_crypto_handshake, sockfd) != 0) {
449       log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
450       if (created_tcp_client) {
451         tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
452       }
453       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Crypto handshake failed");
454     }
455     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully");
456 
457     // Get crypto context after handshake
458     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
459       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
460     }
461   }
462 
463   // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending/receiving
464   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("connection", sockfd, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
465   if (!transport) {
466     log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for TCP");
467     if (created_tcp_client) {
468       tcp_client_destroy(&tcp_client);
469     }
470     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create ACIP transport");
471   }
472 
473   log_info("TCP connection established to %s:%u", effective_address, effective_port);
474   connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
475   ctx->active_transport = transport;
476 
477   // Store tcp_client in context for proper lifecycle management only if we created it locally
478   // If it's pre-created by the framework, the framework will manage its lifecycle
479   if (created_tcp_client) {
480     ctx->tcp_client_instance = tcp_client;
481     log_debug("TCP client instance stored in connection context for cleanup");
482   } else {
483     log_debug("Using framework-managed TCP client, not storing in context");
484   }
485 
486   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
487 }




References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    client_crypto_handshake(), client_crypto_init(),
    client_crypto_set_mode(), connection_state_transition(),
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    platform_sleep_us(), server_connection_set_ip(),
    server_connection_set_port(),
    session_client_like_set_websocket_client(), should_exit(),
    tcp_client_connect(), tcp_client_create(),
    tcp_client_destroy(), tcp_client_get_socket(),
    time_get_realtime_ns(), url_is_tcp(),
    url_is_websocket(), url_parse(), url_parts_destroy(),
    websocket_client_connect(), websocket_client_create(), and
    websocket_client_destroy().






asciichat_error_t
  connection_attempt_websocket (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx, const char *
  ws_url)


Attempt WebSocket connection (ws:// or wss://). Connects to server
    via WebSocket, performs crypto handshake if enabled, and creates ACIP
    transport for protocol communication.


Definition at line 495 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


495                                                                                                       {
496   log_info("=== connection_attempt_websocket CALLED: url='%s' ===", ws_url);
497 
498   if (!ctx || !ws_url) {
499     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
500   }
501 
502   // Check if shutdown was requested (via callback if available)
503   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
504     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection attempt aborted due to shutdown request");
505   }
506 
507   log_info("Attempting WebSocket connection to %s", ws_url);
508 
509   // Transition to attempting state
510   asciichat_error_t result = connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING);
511   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
512     return result;
513   }
514 
515   // Set timeout for this attempt
516   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
517   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
518 
519   // Initialize crypto context if encryption is enabled
520   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
521   if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
522     log_debug("Initializing crypto context for WebSocket...");
523 
524     if (APP_CALLBACK_INT(client_crypto_init) != 0) {
525       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto context");
526       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
527     }
528     log_debug("Crypto context initialized successfully");
529 
530     // Get crypto context if ready
531     if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(crypto_client_is_ready)) {
532       crypto_ctx = APP_CALLBACK_PTR(crypto_client_get_context);
533     }
534   }
535 
536   // Create WebSocket client instance
537   websocket_client_t *ws_client = websocket_client_create("connection");
538   if (!ws_client) {
539     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket client");
540     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
541     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket client creation failed");
542   }
543 
544   // Connect via WebSocket
545   acip_transport_t *transport = websocket_client_connect(ws_client, ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
546   if (!transport) {
547     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
548     websocket_client_destroy(&ws_client);
549     connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_FAILED);
550     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "WebSocket connection failed");
551   }
552 
553   log_info("WebSocket connection established to %s", ws_url);
554   connection_state_transition(ctx, CONN_STATE_CONNECTED);
555   ctx->active_transport = transport;
556   ctx->ws_client_instance = ws_client;
557   log_debug("WebSocket client instance stored in connection context");
558 
559   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
560 }






References client_crypto_init(),
    connection_state_transition(), crypto_client_get_context(),
    crypto_client_is_ready(), should_exit(),
    time_get_realtime_ns(), websocket_client_connect(),
    websocket_client_create(), and websocket_client_destroy().






bool connection_check_timeout (const
  connection_attempt_context_t * ctx)


Check if connection attempt has exceeded timeout.


Definition at line 155 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


155                                                                        {
156   if (!ctx)
157     return false;
158 
159   uint64_t elapsed_ns = time_get_realtime_ns() - ctx->attempt_start_time_ns;
160   bool timeout_exceeded = elapsed_ns > ctx->timeout_ns;
161 
162   if (timeout_exceeded) {
163     char elapsed_str[32], timeout_str[32];
164     time_pretty(elapsed_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
165     time_pretty(ctx->timeout_ns, -1, timeout_str, sizeof(timeout_str));
166     log_warn("Connection timeout exceeded: elapsed %s > %s limit", elapsed_str, timeout_str);
167   }
168 
169   return timeout_exceeded;
170 }






References time_get_realtime_ns(), and
    time_pretty().






void
  connection_context_cleanup (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx)


Cleanup connection attempt context.


Definition at line 102 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


102                                                                    {
103   if (!ctx)
104     return;
105 
106   // Destroy TCP client instance if created
107   if (ctx->tcp_client_instance) {
108     tcp_client_destroy(&ctx->tcp_client_instance);
109     ctx->tcp_client_instance = NULL;
110     log_debug("TCP client instance destroyed");
111   }
112 
113   // Note: Do NOT destroy the WebSocket client instance here. The WebSocket client
114   // is owned by the session layer (passed via config->websocket_client and also
115   // stored in g_websocket_client). The session cleanup will destroy it.
116   // We just clear the reference to prevent double-free.
117   if (ctx->ws_client_instance) {
118     ctx->ws_client_instance = NULL;
119     ctx->active_transport = NULL;
120     log_debug("WebSocket client reference cleared (ownership remains with session)");
121   }
122 
123   // Close active transport if still open (only if not already destroyed by WebSocket client)
124   if (ctx->active_transport) {
125     acip_transport_close(ctx->active_transport);
126     acip_transport_destroy(ctx->active_transport);
127     ctx->active_transport = NULL;
128     log_debug("Transport connection closed");
129   }
130 
131   log_debug("Connection context cleaned up");
132 }






References acip_transport_destroy(), and
    tcp_client_destroy().


Referenced by client_main().






asciichat_error_t
  connection_context_init (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx)


Initialize connection attempt context.


Definition at line 74 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


74                                                                              {
75   if (!ctx) {
76     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Context pointer is NULL");
77   }
78 
79   // Reset context
80   memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(connection_attempt_context_t));
81 
82   // Initialize state
83   ctx->current_state = CONN_STATE_IDLE;
84   ctx->previous_state = CONN_STATE_IDLE;
85 
86   // Initialize timeout
87   ctx->attempt_start_time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
88   ctx->timeout_ns = CONN_TIMEOUT_TCP;
89 
90   // Initialize counters
91   ctx->reconnect_attempt = 1;
92   ctx->total_transitions = 0;
93 
94   log_debug("Connection context initialized");
95 
96   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
97 }

const
  char * connection_state_name (connection_state_t state)


Get human-readable state name for logging.


Definition at line 50 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


50                                                             {
51   switch (state) {
52   case CONN_STATE_IDLE:
53     return "IDLE";
54   case CONN_STATE_ATTEMPTING:
55     return "ATTEMPTING";
56   case CONN_STATE_CONNECTED:
57     return "CONNECTED";
58   case CONN_STATE_DISCONNECTED:
59     return "DISCONNECTED";
60   case CONN_STATE_FAILED:
61     return "FAILED";
62   default:
63     return "UNKNOWN";
64   }
65 }






Referenced by connection_state_transition().






asciichat_error_t
  connection_state_transition (connection_attempt_context_t * ctx,
  connection_state_t new_state)


Transition to next connection state with validation.


Definition at line 137 of file
  connection_attempt.c.


137                                                                                                                {
138   if (!ctx) {
139     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Context pointer is NULL");
140   }
141 
142   // Store previous state
143   ctx->previous_state = ctx->current_state;
144   ctx->current_state = new_state;
145   ctx->total_transitions++;
146 
147   log_debug("State transition: %s → %s", connection_state_name(ctx->previous_state), connection_state_name(new_state));
148 
149   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
150 }

  session_client_like_set_websocket_client (struct websocket_client *

    ascii-chat
